summary: 10 concrete recommendations surfaced from 22 scraped HN stories.

items.name: Project Gutenberg
items.summary: Free ebook library with better book feeds and text-friendly reading.
items.why_included: Commenters praised the new-books feed and the usefulness of PG texts with modern readers and AI.
items.evidence: HN commenters called the new-books feed a goldmine and mentioned using AI models and e-readers with Gutenberg texts.

items.name: Zulip
items.summary: Open-source team chat that a longtime user still relies on daily.
items.why_included: A 13-year Zulip user singled it out as one of their favorite pieces of software.
items.evidence: The thread included a direct praise note from a long-time user saying they use Zulip daily.

items.name: Image-blaster
items.source_story_title: Image-blaster: Creates 3D environments, SFX, and meshes from a single image
items.summary: Single-image tool that generates 3D scenes, meshes, and SFX.
items.why_included: Commenters reacted positively and compared it with adjacent image-to-3D experiments.
items.evidence: Replies called it cool as hell and linked to Photosynth, WorldLabs, and Uthana as nearby references.

items.name: Explore Wikipedia Like a Windows XP Desktop
items.source_story_title: Explore Wikipedia Like a Windows XP Desktop
items.summary: Retro Wikipedia browser wrapped in a Windows XP-style desktop.
items.why_included: The thread was full of instant praise from people who wanted to use it right away.
items.evidence: Commenters said things like “I love it”, “Very cool”, “Super nice”, and “Impressive”.

items.name: Zenith
items.source_story_title: Zenith: a live local-first fixed viewport planetarium
items.summary: Local-first planetarium that makes Earth's rotation feel immediate.
items.why_included: Users reacted strongly to how visceral the fixed-viewport experience felt.
items.evidence: Comments described the effect as amazing and said it made Earth’s motion feel immediate and visceral.

items.name: Trolley
items.source_story_title: Feedr v0.8.0 – a TUI RSS reader, now read the full article from your terminal
items.summary: Packaging helper for making Feedr easier to ship to non-technical users.
items.why_included: A commenter explicitly recommended Trolley as the packaging path for the app.
items.evidence: The strongest concrete recommendation in the thread was to use Trolley to package Feedr for non-technical users.

items.name: Radicle
items.source_story_title: Radicle: Sovereign {code forge} built on Git
items.summary: Git-based code forge built for sovereign, decentralized collaboration.
items.why_included: Commenters focused on the absence of a central operator and the ownership model.
items.evidence: Replies emphasized that no single entity controls the network or user data.

items.name: ASCII by Jason Scott
items.summary: ASCII and manuals archive from Jason Scott’s textfiles project.
items.why_included: HN readers linked the archive, stream, and Patreon while praising the long-running collection.
items.evidence: Commenters pointed to the manuals archive and highlighted the scale of the collection over time.

items.name: Steve Jobs in Exile
items.source_story_title: A new book on Steve Jobs at NeXT
items.summary: Book on Steve Jobs's NeXT years and the discipline they forced.
items.why_included: HN users tied the NeXT era to product focus, constraint, and the road back to Apple.
items.evidence: Comments said the NeXT years gave Jobs space to rethink product focus and learn from failure.

items.name: High dimensional geometry is transforming the MRI industry
items.source_story_title: High dimensional geometry is transforming the MRI industry (2017) [pdf]
items.summary: Paper on high-dimensional geometry’s role in faster MRI reconstruction.
items.why_included: Commenters treated it as a useful review of compressed-sensing MRI ideas.
items.evidence: Replies discussed weaker magnets, information gain, and iterative linear-program approaches.
